subject: Product Management
title: Story Mapping
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Story Mapping.

hook.text: Jeff Patton invented story mapping to solve a common problem: flat backlogs lose context about the user's actual journey.

buildup.text: A story map lays out the user's journey left to right, with each step broken into stories arranged top to bottom by priority.

discovery.text: The top row is the 'walking skeleton' — the minimum viable journey. Each row below adds richness and polish.

twist.text: Story maps make it obvious when a release covers one part of the journey deeply but skips critical steps entirely.

climax.text: Teams draw a horizontal line across the map to define each release. Everything above the line ships; everything below waits.

punchline.text: Map the journey. Draw the line. Ship what's above it.

quiz.question: What problem does story mapping solve?
options: Flat backlogs lose context about the user journey — story maps restore it
Story mapping replaces all other prioritization frameworks
It eliminates the need for user research
